diagrams.title: Normalization Hierarchy Pyramid
diagrams.description: Shows progression from unnormalized to BCNF with each level building on previous
elements: Second Normal Form (2NF) - No partial dependencies
Third Normal Form (3NF) - No transitive dependencies
Boyce-Codd Normal Form (BCNF) - All determinants are superkeys
diagrams.purpose: Visualize that each normal form builds upon previous forms, and BCNF is the strictest requiring all determinants to be superkeys.

diagrams.title: Functional Dependency Types
diagrams.description: Illustrates different types of dependencies that are eliminated at each normal form level
elements: Full Functional Dependency (all attributes depend on full key)
diagrams.purpose: Understand what each normal form targets - partial dependencies in 2NF, transitive dependencies in 3NF.

diagrams.title: Anomaly Examples Flowchart
diagrams.description: Shows the three types of anomalies and their consequences in a poorly designed database
elements: Insertion Anomaly - Cannot add without unrelated data
Update Anomaly - Must update multiple places
Deletion Anomaly - Lose related data on deletion
diagrams.purpose: Helps remember why normalization is necessary - all three anomalies stem from poor design and data redundancy.

diagrams.title: Key Types in Normalization
diagrams.description: Different types of keys and their roles in normalization
elements: Prime Attribute - Part of any candidate key
Non-Prime Attribute - Not in any candidate key
diagrams.purpose: Understanding keys is essential for determining normal forms - prime attributes play special role in 3NF vs BCNF distinction.
